project_summary: A comprehensive online examination platform with built-in proctoring, role-aware exam delivery, collaborative teacher assignment, live exam/question refresh, folder-based question banks, permission-aligned user management, entity-aware soft-delete/restore capabilities, and analytics-rich dashboards. Features RBAC (Admin, Examiner, Student), password-gated exam start/resume, assigned-examiner schedule ownership, server-authoritative attempt timers with ends_at tracking, browser-based focus/visibility proctoring prompts, automatic MCQ grading, role-scoped Recently Deleted recovery, and shared student/teacher/admin reporting views. Built with FastAPI (async Python) backend, PostgreSQL database, and a Vite-compiled TypeScript + vanilla CSS frontend served via Nginx. Orchestrated with Docker Compose.

architecture.pattern: Layered Architecture (Presentation → API → Service → Repository → Database)
architecture.state_management: Server-side session via JWT tokens; client-side localStorage for token persistence
architecture.notes: Services and repositories directories still exist but remain unused — business logic lives directly in API route handlers. Frontend remains a 4-page architecture, but exam start/resume is now password-gated through a server-verified attempt access token, schedule/timer fields are managed only by assigned examiners or admins, attempt timers live on the attempt row via ends_at/last_opened_at instead of the client clock alone, and proctoring records acknowledged focus-loss incidents without terminating the attempt. The browser-based prompt now asks students to review any other open tabs or windows while clearly documenting that browsers only expose focus/visibility signals, not full window enumeration. Question-bank and exam-detail serializers still rely on eager-loaded folder owner/share/question-count relationships, and student exam lists no longer expose full question content before an attempt exists.

nodes.title: OEPS
nodes.summary: Online Examination and Proctoring System — full-stack web application for conducting secure online exams with proctoring

nodes.title: Backend (FastAPI)
nodes.summary: Python 3.11 FastAPI application with async SQLAlchemy ORM, JWT auth, and Alembic migrations. Serves REST API at /api/v1.

nodes.title: Frontend (Vite + TypeScript)
nodes.summary: Multi-page frontend with HTML5, TypeScript modules, and vanilla CSS. Built with Vite, served by Nginx in production. Four pages: Login, Dashboard, Exam, Result.

nodes.title: Database (PostgreSQL 15)
nodes.summary: PostgreSQL 15 Alpine container with persistent volume. Stores users, exams, questions, attempts, responses, proctoring logs, and results.

nodes.title: Docker Compose Orchestration
nodes.summary: Orchestrates 3 services: db (postgres:15-alpine), backend (FastAPI), frontend (Nginx). Backend mounts app directory for hot-reload. Frontend uses multi-stage build.

nodes.title: App Entry Point (main.py)
nodes.summary: Creates FastAPI app instance, configures CORS middleware, and mounts the v1 API router at /api/v1.

nodes.title: Settings & Configuration
nodes.summary: Pydantic BaseSettings class loading from environment variables. Supports both individual POSTGRES_* vars and single DATABASE_URL. Constructs async SQLAlchemy URI with postgresql+asyncpg scheme.

nodes.title: Security Module
nodes.summary: JWT token creation (jose), bcrypt password hashing/verification (passlib). Creates access tokens with user ID, role, and expiry.

nodes.title: API Dependencies (Auth Guards)
nodes.summary: FastAPI dependency injection for authentication. Provides get_current_user (JWT decode), get_current_active_user, get_current_active_superuser (Admin only), and get_current_active_examiner (Admin + Examiner).

nodes.title: Database Session Manager
nodes.summary: Creates the async SQLAlchemy engine and AsyncSessionLocal factory lazily so helper imports and focused tests do not require asyncpg unless a real DB session is requested. Provides get_db() async generator for dependency injection.

nodes.title: Alembic Migrations
nodes.summary: Async Alembic migration environment. The repo now contains a committed bootstrap revision (20260424_01) that creates the full schema on fresh databases and conditionally adds the exam password/schedule audit and attempt timing columns on existing deployments.

nodes.title: Startup Script
nodes.summary: Bash script that waits for PostgreSQL, auto-generates migrations if needed, runs alembic upgrade head, creates default superuser, then starts uvicorn with hot-reload.

nodes.title: Superuser Seeder
nodes.summary: Python script that creates default admin user (admin@example.com / password) if not exists. Uses sync-compatible asyncio loop with AsyncSessionLocal.

nodes.title: User Model
nodes.summary: SQLAlchemy model for users table. Fields: id, name, email (unique), password_hash, role (UserRole enum: ADMIN/EXAMINER/STUDENT), is_active, created_at. Relationships to exams_created, assignments, attempts, owned question folders, and shared-folder links. The exams_created relationship is now pinned explicitly to exams.created_by because Exam also tracks schedule_updated_by for schedule/timer audit metadata, and user snapshots still preserve password_hash for reliable trash restores.

nodes.title: Exam Model
nodes.summary: SQLAlchemy model for exams table. Fields now include password_hash plus schedule_updated_by/schedule_updated_at alongside id, title, instructions, status (ExamStatus enum: DRAFT/SCHEDULED/LIVE/CLOSED/RESULT_PUBLISHED), start_time, duration_minutes, created_by (FK users), and created_at. Relationships to creator, questions (via ExamQuestion), student assignments, teacher assignments, and attempts remain, but schedule/timer/password writes now audit back to the assigned-examiner/admin path while async exam serialization continues to rely on eager-loaded question-folder metadata and question counts.

nodes.title: Question Model
nodes.summary: SQLAlchemy question-bank model layer. Questions store creator + folder context alongside type, prompt, options, correct_option, marks, and created_at. The same file defines question_folders and question_folder_shares for owner-managed banks, explicit examiner sharing, admin default visibility, and restore-complete question/folder snapshots with exam-link context. Dashboard question-bank serialization now depends on eager-loaded folder questions and share links so folder metadata can be returned without async lazy-load crashes.

nodes.title: ExamQuestion Association
nodes.summary: Many-to-many association table linking exams to questions with an order_index field. Composite PK (exam_id, question_id). This association sits on the critical path for exam-detail refresh after add/remove question operations and now depends on the eager-loaded folder metadata attached to each linked question.

nodes.title: Assignment Model
nodes.summary: Exam assignment model layer. The file now contains both student assignments (assignments table) and teacher collaboration assignments (teacher_assignments table) so admins and examiners can control who can view and operate an exam.

nodes.title: Attempt Model
nodes.summary: SQLAlchemy model for attempts table. Fields now include ends_at and last_opened_at alongside id, exam_id (FK), student_id (FK), status (AttemptStatus: IN_PROGRESS/SUBMITTED/EVALUATED), started_at, and submitted_at. Relationships to exam, student, responses, logs, and result remain. The attempt row is now the timer source of truth so a valid started attempt can continue until ends_at even if later schedule changes occur, and trash snapshots/restores preserve the timing fields.

nodes.title: Response Model
nodes.summary: SQLAlchemy model for responses table. Stores student answers per question per attempt. Fields: id, attempt_id (FK), question_id (FK), answer, marks_awarded.

nodes.title: ProctoringLog Model
nodes.summary: SQLAlchemy model for proctoring_logs table. Logs browser-derived integrity incidents during exam. Fields: id, attempt_id (FK), event_type, severity, metadata_info, created_at. Focus-loss incidents are now recorded as flag events such as TRUSTED_CONTEXT_EXIT without changing the attempt status or terminating the exam.

nodes.title: Result Model
nodes.summary: SQLAlchemy model for results table. Fields: id, attempt_id (FK, unique), total_score, max_score, published_at. One-to-one with Attempt.

nodes.title: Trash Model
nodes.summary: SQLAlchemy model for trash table. Stores soft-deleted users, exams, questions, folders, and attempts as serialized JSON snapshots. Snapshot metadata now drives role-aware listing, search, restore, and permanent-delete rules instead of relying only on the deleting user.

nodes.title: API v1 Router
nodes.summary: Central APIRouter aggregating 6 sub-routers: /auth, /users, /exams, /attempts, /reports, and /trash. Mounted at /api/v1.

nodes.title: Auth API (/auth)
nodes.summary: POST /auth/login — OAuth2 password flow. Validates email/password, returns JWT access token with user ID and role.

nodes.title: Users API (/users)
nodes.summary: Role-aware user management API. Admins keep full control, while examiners can list/search all users, create student or examiner accounts, update peer student/examiner access, toggle access for peer student/examiner accounts, and never modify self or admin access. Admin-only delete still moves users into trash, and responses include per-row capability flags for the dashboard.

nodes.title: Exams API (/exams)
nodes.summary: Exam and question-bank management API. Supports folder CRUD, question CRUD, folder sharing to examiners, creator-aware question-bank visibility (including admin default visibility for examiner-owned banks), question-bank search, exam CRUD, question-to-exam linking, collaborative teacher/student assignment, safe delete guards when submissions exist, and trash snapshots for exams, folders, and questions. Schedule-sensitive fields (start_time, duration_minutes, status, password) are now writable only by assigned examiners or admins, student list/detail payloads no longer expose question content before an attempt exists, and exam creation/update enforces password presence plus schedule audit metadata.

nodes.title: Attempts API (/attempts)
nodes.summary: Attempt lifecycle API. Students can start or resume attempts only when the exam is assigned, the first-open window is still valid, the password is correct, and the exam contains questions. Start/resume now issues an exam access token, attempts own their authoritative ends_at/last_opened_at values, expired in-progress attempts auto-transition to SUBMITTED on read/write, response/proctoring/submit routes require the signed exam token while an attempt is active, and proctoring incidents are recorded without terminating the attempt.

nodes.title: Reports API (/reports)
nodes.summary: Advanced analytics API for admins, exam creators/collaborators, and students. GET /dashboard returns scoped portfolio KPIs, status mix, score bands, risk mix, activity timeline, and top-exam rankings. GET /exam/{exam_id}/summary and /analytics return per-exam funnels, distributions, question insights, leaderboard rows, and proctoring breakdowns. GET /attempt/{attempt_id}/analytics and /student/me power shared result analytics across all roles.

nodes.title: Trash API (/trash)
nodes.summary: Recently Deleted API for typed listing, filtering, search, restore, and permanent delete across users, exams, questions, folders, and attempts. Authorization is entity-aware: admins manage everything, while examiners only see and act on items their live delete permissions would have covered. Exam restores now carry password_hash and schedule audit fields forward, and attempt restores preserve ends_at plus last_opened_at so timer behavior stays consistent after recovery.

nodes.title: Pydantic Schemas
nodes.summary: Request/response validation schemas for users, exams, questions, attempts, tokens, and trash. Exam payloads now expose requires_password, question_count, and schedule/timer capability flags without leaking password data, while attempt payloads carry ends_at, last_opened_at, remaining_seconds, server_time, and the start-only exam_access_token needed to keep active exam calls password-gated.

nodes.title: Test Suite
nodes.summary: Pytest coverage remains lightweight, but now includes focused helper and route-unit coverage for assigned-examiner schedule access, password-gated start/resume, authoritative attempt timing, non-terminating proctor flags, user-management protections, shared question-bank access, trash authorization, and query eager-load regression assertions. The DB session factory is now lazy so these focused tests can run without importing asyncpg unless a real DB session is requested.

nodes.title: Login Screen
nodes.summary: Full-viewport centered card with email/password form. Submits to /api/v1/auth/login as URL-encoded form data. Stores JWT in localStorage, redirects to /dashboard.html.

nodes.title: Dashboard Screen
nodes.summary: Role-aware operations workspace with a responsive sidebar, live-refreshing exam/results/report views, examiner-accessible user management, protected admin/self access rows, folder-sharing modals, folder/question capability-driven actions, submission deletion from exam attempts, and Recently Deleted search/filter/action states driven by backend permissions. Student start/resume now opens a password modal that only establishes access through the server, exam cards hide schedule controls unless can_manage_schedule is true, and staff attempt tables show authoritative remaining time from attempt.remaining_seconds.

nodes.title: Exam Screen
nodes.summary: Two-column exam workspace with question navigator, live save state, password-gated resume support, server-authoritative countdown display, manual/auto submission, focus/visibility proctoring prompts, and polling-based live exam synchronization. The screen now preserves draft answers and focus state when question wording/order/availability changes mid-attempt, and flagged focus-loss incidents keep the exam active instead of terminating it.

nodes.title: Result Screen
nodes.summary: Result and analytics view for a single attempt. Shows score/status plus answered-rate, time-spent, integrity breakdown, and per-question analytics. Shared attempt-report permissions let students, assigned teachers, creators, and admins open the same richer report page.

nodes.title: API Client (apiFetch)
nodes.summary: Centralized fetch wrapper. Adds Authorization Bearer token, conditionally sets JSON content type, handles 401 redirect to login, tolerates 204/text payloads, and normalizes network/API failures into clearer frontend errors.

nodes.title: Proctor Class
nodes.summary: Client-side proctoring module. Listens to visibilitychange, blur, and focus events, prompts the student to review open tabs/windows when trusted focus is lost, records a TRUSTED_CONTEXT_EXIT incident through the attempts log API, shows a warning flash, and cleans up bound listeners correctly when the exam flow ends. The prompt is intentionally limited to supported focus/visibility signals because browsers cannot enumerate every other tab or window.

nodes.title: Frontend Utilities
nodes.summary: Shared utility functions for token persistence, adaptive time formatting, debounce, toast rendering, HTML escaping, and status-badge mapping used across auth, dashboard, exam, and result flows.

nodes.title: CSS Design System
nodes.summary: Shared light-theme design system with responsive auth/dashboard/exam layouts, modern card and form primitives, and dedicated report visual components such as KPI cards, donut indicators, bar rows, timeline charts, and insight panels. Dashboard stat cards now use consistent SVG icon slots with color-matched tints, and folder cards expose keyboard-focusable open states that match the existing card system.

nodes.title: Nginx Reverse Proxy
nodes.summary: Serves static files from Vite build output. Proxies /api/ requests to backend:8000. Handles error pages.

nodes.title: Authentication Flow
nodes.summary: Login form → POST /api/v1/auth/login (URL-encoded) → backend validates email/password → returns JWT → stored in localStorage → subsequent API calls attach Bearer token → deps.py decodes JWT → returns User → role check guards endpoints.

nodes.title: Exam Lifecycle Flow
nodes.summary: Admin/examiner creates exams and foldered questions → question banks can be shared with examiners while admins retain default visibility → Question Bank folder selection stays inline on the dashboard and reads /exams/questions?folder_id=... → teachers and students are assigned → exam detail draws its addable-question source from the same accessible question-bank query and now surfaces source failures inline instead of silently appearing empty → student sees only assigned/eligible exams and must enter the exam password on the dashboard to start or resume → the first valid open creates or refreshes an attempt whose ends_at timer becomes the source of truth, so an in-progress attempt can continue until timer expiry even after the original schedule window closes → active attempts auto-save while polling for live question changes → focus-loss incidents are flagged without terminating the attempt → examiner/admin evaluates or deletes submissions into trash → exams, folders, questions, and attempts restore through Recently Deleted, while exam deletion is blocked until submissions are cleared.

nodes.title: Proctoring Flow
nodes.summary: Proctor class instantiates on exam page → listens to visibilitychange, blur, and focus → when trusted focus is lost, the student is prompted to review any other open tabs/windows within browser capability limits → confirmation POSTs a TRUSTED_CONTEXT_EXIT incident to /attempts/{id}/log with metadata → backend stores the flag without changing the attempt status and later aggregates it into dashboard and per-exam analytics for event breakdowns, risk bands, and integrity alert counts.

nodes.title: Services Layer (Empty)
nodes.summary: Empty directory intended for business logic service classes. Currently all logic lives in API route handlers.

nodes.title: Repositories Layer (Empty)
nodes.summary: Empty directory intended for data access repository classes. Currently all DB queries are inline in route handlers.

edges.label: loads User by ID from token
exam/question bank creation + safe delete guards
restores attempts, responses, logs, and results

open_questions: Services and repositories directories are empty — business logic lives fully in route handlers. Should it be refactored?

Focused helper and route-unit tests now cover schedule ownership, password-gated start/resume, authoritative attempt timing, and non-terminating proctor flags, but end-to-end API/UI permission tests still need a DB-backed harness and async drivers in local environments.
Browser proctoring cannot enumerate every open tab or window. The current warning flow relies on supported focus/visibility signals plus student confirmation.
Docker startup still logs an Alembic revision mismatch (missing revision 371f4658a015) even though the application boots.
Frontend timer display now compensates with server_time and ends_at, but background tab throttling can still delay repaint; the backend remains authoritative for expiry and submission.

Shared folder collaborators can edit existing shared questions, but they still cannot create new questions inside another owner's folder. Confirm whether question banks should remain owner-managed or become fully collaborative.
